reasoning: The base rate of the U.S. invading a sovereign nation like Venezuela to establish territorial control is extremely low. Even if there are high geopolitical tensions, launching a full-scale ground invasion would require a massive buildup of forces and logistical preparation that would take many months and be highly visible. With only a little over two months left in 2025 (October 26 to December 31), there is insufficient time for such a military mobilization to occur without already being completely obvious and pricing the market much higher. Polymarket's 10% price on October 16 likely reflects a pronounced long-shot bias, which often keeps highly unlikely tail-risk events trading at inflated values (typically around 5-10%). Because there have been no indicators of an imminent US ground invasion by late October, the objective probability of this occurring by year-end is practically zero. Thus, I am assigning the lowest valid probability of 0.02.
